Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 2, 050 [recipes][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

na₄ZA.GIN₃ na₄AŠ.GI₃.GI₃ na₄UGU.AŠ.GI₃.GI₃ na₄ŠIM.BI.ZI.DA IM.SIG₇.SIG₇

(o 1) You cook lapis lazuli, ašgigû-stone, ...-stone, antimony, dāmātu-paste [...] (and) ... equally in šīrīsu-beer. Afterwards, you cook (them) in milk. You ... the dāmātu-paste (and) .... You ... the būšānu-plant, after them all. You dissolve in ...-oil and wax. (This is) the tested ointment for the birratu-disease.

o 22

[na₄bu]-⸢qu₃?⸣-ma-tu₄ mal₂-ma-liš ina ši-ri-su ŠEG₆šal EGIR ina GA ŠEG₆šal1

o 33

⸢IM.SIG₇⸣.SIG₇ na₄⸢bu⸣-qu₃?-mat ŠUMaḫ u₂ḪAB EGIR gab-bi-šu₂-nu

o 44

ŠUMaḫ ina I₃.EGIR u DUḪ.LAL₃ tu-la-ma te-qit la-tik₂-tu₄ ša₂ bir-rat

o 55

2 gir₂-e IM.SIG₇.SIG₇ 2 gir₂-e kur-ru-ku-um-mu gir₂-u₂ U₂ BABBAR

(o 5) You ... together 2 girû (1/24 shekel) of dāmātu-paste, 2 girû of kurrukummu, 1 girû of white-plant (and) 1 girû of algae. You compact blood of male and female partridge. You anoint his eyes with the ointment for the birratu-disease (and with) ditto (the ointment of) the tābīlu-disease. On the fourth day, when he becomes feverish, you pour milk on his eyes. After the milk (has been poured), you anoint his eyes dry with ashar-stone.

o 66

gir₂-u₂ KA tam-ti₃ 1niš ŠUMaḫ UŠ₂ BURU₅.ḪABRUD.DAmušen NITA₂ u MUNUS

o 77

ta-se-eḫ-ḫu te-qit ša₂ bir-rat IGI-MIN-šu₂! KI.MIN<(te-qit)> ša₂ ta-bi-lu IGI-MIN-šu₂ MAR2

o 88

ina u₄-mu 4šu₂ GA ina KUM₂mi-šu₂ a-na IGI-MIN-šu₂ ŠUBdi EGIR GA

o 99

na₄as-ḫar ta-bi-lu IGI-MIN-šu₂ MAR

o 1010

1/2 GIN₂ U₅ ARGABmušen 1/2 GIN₂ U₂ BABBAR 15 ŠE MUN eme-⸢sal⸣-li₃

(o 10) You dry in the shade 1/2 shekel of bat's guano, 1/2 shekel of white-plant, 15 grains of mēsallu-salt (and) a būšānu-plant. You dissolve (them). You pound (them) in filtered oil if (it is during) summer (or) in butter if (it is during) winter. You anoint the tested 'ointment of Hammu-rabi'. When you have done it, (the patient) should not consume brewer's beer, he should regularly anoint (his eye and) stay in darkness.

o 1111

u₂ḪAB ina GISSU ḪAD₂.DU tu-lam šum₄-ma KUM₂-MEŠ ina I₃ ḫal-ṣa

o 1212

šum₄-ma EN.TE.NA ina I₃.NUN SUD₂ MAR MARtu₂ ša₂ mḫa-mu-ra-bi

o 1313

la-tik₂-tu₂ e-nu-ma DU₃šu₂ KAŠ lu₂KURUN.NA la i-lem

o 1414

li-te-<ne>-qi₂ ina KUKKU₂ li-šib

o 1515

* NA IGI-MIN-šu₂ i-bar-ru-ra 1/2 GIN₂ U₂ BABBAR 2 GIN₂ U₅ ARGABmušen

(o 15) (If) a person's eyes become dimmed: you pound together, in ordinary oil, 1/2 shekels of white-plant, 2 shekels of bat's guano, 15 grains of alum, 15 grains of mēsallu-salt, 1/8 (shekel) of pith of kanaktu-tree (and then) you anoint his eyes.

o 1616

15 ŠE na₄gab-u₂ 15 ŠE MUN eme-sal-li₃ bit-qa I₃.UDU šimGIG

Reverse
r 1r 1

[1]niš ina I₃ gu-un-nu SUD₂ IGI-MIN-šu₂ MAR

r 22

[...] IGI-MIN-šu₂ i-bar-ru-ra u ER₂ u₂-kal-la U₂ BABBAR LAL₃

(r 2) [...] eyes become dimmed and contain tear(s): you pound white-plant, honey (and) nīnû-plant in oil (and then) you anoint.

r 33

⸢u₂⸣KUR.RA ina I₃ SUD₂ MAR

r 44

* NA IGI-MIN-šu₂ i-bar-ru-ra šimSES U₂ BABBAR U₅ ARGABmušen

(r 4) (If) a person's eyes become dimmed: you pound myrrh, white-plant, guano of bat in butter (and then) you anoint his eyes.

r 55

ina I₃.NUN.NA SUD₂ IGI-MIN-šu₂ MAR

r 66

* NA IGI-MIN-šu₂ ER₂ u₂-kal-la saḫ-le₁₀ ṭe₄-ne₂-e-tu₂ ina GA ŠIDaš

(r 6) (If) a person's eyes continually contain tear(s): you knead milled cress in milk (and then) you bandage his eyes.

r 77

IGI-MIN-šu₂ LA₂id

r 88

* NA IGI-MIN-šu₂ LU₃.LU₃ḫa LAL₃ I₃.NUN.NA I₃.SAG ḫal-ṣa 1niš KUM₂.KUM₂am ta-ša₂-ḫal ana IGI-MIN-šu₂ ŠUB3

(r 8) (If) a person's eyes are troubled: you heat up together honey, butter (and) filtered fine quality oil, (then) you filter (and) pour (it) into his eyes.

r 99

* NA EME-šu₂ GIG IM.KAL.LA.GUG URUDU SUMUN ina I₃.NUN.NA ḪI.ḪI

(r 9) (If) a person's tongue is ill: you mix kalgukku-paste (and) old copper in butter (and then) you anoint him. Tested for the mouth.

r 1010

ŠEŠ₂-su ša? KA? lat-ku

r 1111

A.GAR₅ ina A-MEŠ šeGIG.BA ta-sal-laq SUD₂ ina qup?-pat? ša₂ GI ŠUB-šu₂

(r 11) You boil lead in wheat-water. You pound. You pour it (the treatment) with a spatula of reed. It is good for widening the eyes.

r 1212

a-na DAGALeš IGI-MIN SIG₅

1{na₄}bu-qu₃?-ma-tu₄ could also be read {na₄}pu-zir₃-ma-tu₄. Cf. also line o.3

2"ta-se-eh-hu" could be a verb of the same root as "sihītu" which could be a process for treating barley

3hal-ṣa is written on the preceding ruling above I₃.SAG
